appraisal_events.evidence: « C'était bien cela, la noblesse, l'intelligence de la diction. » / « la Berma y était aussi sublime que dans Phèdre » / « la Berma savait introduire ces vastes images de douleur, de noblesse, de passion, qui étaient ses chefs-d'oeuvre à elle »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator explicitly reinterprets his earlier disappointment as true admiration and presents Berma’s artistry as autonomous and sublime across roles, elevating her interpretive genius beyond the playwright’s text.

status_effects.explanation: Locally, Berma’s standing rises decisively as the narrator affirms her as a creator of a 'second work' and recognizes her noble, powerful diction and interpretive genius.
